notes: Removed vague/prospective candidates: tariff_policy (says 'could lead to', no measured impact), customer_traffic (mentioned only in context of balancing pricing, not measured), menu_diversity (mentioned as competitive positioning, not as a measured driver), north_italia_average_check_lunch (subsumed by separate lunch/dinner check data), honeymoon_sales_effect (vague about what drives the phenomenon)
Kept company-wide and brand-specific metrics as separate drivers per R9 (e.g., same_store_sales, north_italia_same_store_sales, flower_child_same_store_sales, frc_same_store_sales)
All evidence from single 10-K event dated 2026-02-23, part 1 of 6 (Business section)
